annotations.label: Affine space (informal)

anchor.snippet: an affine space is a geometric structure that generalizes some of the properties of Euclidean spaces

annotations.note: `AddTorsor V P` (notation `AffineSpace V P`) is exactly an affine space as a torsor of a vector space action.

annotations.label: Affine subspace by translation

anchor.snippet: Adding a fixed vector to the elements of a linear subspace (vector subspace) of a vector space produces an affine subspace of the vector space

annotations.note: `AffineSubspace.mk' p direction` is the translate of a submodule by a point, i.e. `p + direction`.

annotations.label: Affine subspace as solution set

anchor.snippet: In finite dimensions, such an affine subspace is the solution set of an inhomogeneous linear system

annotations.note: Affine subspaces as cosets are formalized via `mk'`, but the identification with solution sets of inhomogeneous linear systems is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Dimension of an affine space

anchor.snippet: The dimension of an affine space is defined as the dimension of the vector space of its translations

annotations.note: The direction submodule is defined and `finrank` of it is used, but there is no dedicated `dim`/`finrank` definition for an affine space itself.

annotations.label: Affine line, plane, hyperplane

anchor.snippet: An affine space of dimension one is an affine line

annotations.note: Lines exist as `line[k, p₁, p₂]` (affineSpan of a pair), but affine line/plane/hyperplane are not defined as a dimension-based classification.

annotations.label: Affine space as forgetting the origin

anchor.snippet: an affine space is what is left of a vector space after one has forgotten which point is the origin

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` is precisely a vector space with no distinguished origin.

annotations.label: Affine combination

anchor.snippet: the values of affine combinations , defined as linear combinations in which the sum of the coefficients is 1

annotations.note: `Finset.affineCombination` is the weighted combination of points used with weights summing to 1.

annotations.label: Set with affine structure

anchor.snippet: A set with an affine structure is an affine space

annotations.note: The affine structure on a set is the `AddTorsor`/`AffineSpace` typeclass.

annotations.label: Affine space (formal)

anchor.snippet: An affine space is a set A together with a vector space

annotations.note: `AddTorsor G P` is a set `P` with an additive group `G` acting freely and transitively via `+ᵥ`/`-ᵥ`.

annotations.label: Group action axioms

anchor.snippet: The first two properties are simply defining properties of a (right) group action

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` extends `AddAction`, whose axioms `zero_vadd` and `add_vadd` are the group-action laws.

annotations.label: Subtraction property

anchor.snippet: For every a , b in A , there exists a unique

annotations.note: `eq_vadd_iff_vsub_eq` expresses that the unique vector with `b = v +ᵥ a` is `v = b -ᵥ a`.

annotations.label: Principal homogeneous space

anchor.snippet: an affine space is a principal homogeneous space for the action of the additive group of a vector space

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` is Mathlib's name for a torsor / principal homogeneous space of an additive group action.

annotations.label: Subtraction of points

anchor.snippet: the definition of subtraction for any given ordered pair

annotations.note: The `-ᵥ` (`VSub`) operation of `AddTorsor`, characterized by `vsub_vadd : (p₁ -ᵥ p₂) +ᵥ p₂ = p₁`.

annotations.label: Weyl's axioms

anchor.snippet: called Weyl 's axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib's `AddTorsor` captures the same structure but is axiomatized via a group action; Weyl's axioms are not separately given nor proved equivalent.

annotations.label: Parallelogram property

anchor.snippet: The parallelogram property is satisfied in affine spaces

annotations.note: Vector-subtraction cancellation lemmas (`vsub_add_vsub_cancel`, `vsub_sub_vsub_cancel_right`) give the parallelogram identities but it is not stated as a named property.

annotations.label: Equivalent definition via Weyl's axioms

anchor.snippet: Affine spaces can be equivalently defined as a point set A

annotations.note: The torsor definition is the only one in Mathlib; the equivalence with a subtraction-based axiomatization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine subspace

anchor.snippet: An affine subspace (also called, in some contexts, a linear variety , a flat

annotations.note: `AffineSubspace k P` is a subset closed under the affine combination operation `c • (p₁ -ᵥ p₂) +ᵥ p₃`.

annotations.label: Form of affine subspaces

anchor.snippet: The affine subspaces of A are the subsets of A of the form

annotations.note: `mk'_eq` shows every nonempty affine subspace equals `mk' p s.direction`, i.e. `p + direction`.

annotations.label: Direction and parallelism

anchor.snippet: The linear subspace associated with an affine subspace is often called its direction

annotations.note: `AffineSubspace.direction` is the submodule (`vectorSpan`) of differences of points of the subspace.

annotations.label: Generalized Playfair's axiom

anchor.snippet: This implies the following generalization of Playfair's axiom

annotations.note: `mk' p direction` produces the subspace through `p` with a given direction, but the unique-parallel (Playfair) statement is not packaged as a theorem.

annotations.label: Translation preserves parallelism

anchor.snippet: maps any affine subspace to a parallel subspace

annotations.note: `AffineSubspace.Parallel` is defined as one subspace being a translate of the other, directly encoding this.

annotations.label: Parallel for nested directions

anchor.snippet: The term parallel is also used for two affine subspaces

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Parallel` requires equal directions; the weaker notion (one direction contained in the other) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine map

anchor.snippet: an affine map or affine homomorphism from A to B is a map

annotations.note: `AffineMap k P1 P2` (notation `P1 →ᵃ[k] P2`) is a map with an associated linear map satisfying `map_vadd'`.

annotations.label: Affine map determined by one point

anchor.snippet: f is completely defined by its value on a single point and the associated linear map

annotations.note: `ext_linear` shows two affine maps agreeing in linear part and at one point are equal; `AffineMap.mk'` builds a map from this data.

annotations.label: Affine transformation / endomorphism

anchor.snippet: An affine transformation or endomorphism of an affine space

annotations.note: An affine endomorphism is `AffineMap k P P`; invertible ones are `AffineEquiv k P P`.

anchors.snippet: An affine transformation or endomorphism of an affine space

annotations.label: Translations as affine maps

anchor.snippet: One important family of examples is the translations

annotations.note: `AffineEquiv.constVAdd v` is the translation `p ↦ v +ᵥ p` as an affine equivalence (identity linear part).

anchors.snippet: One important family of examples is the translations

annotations.label: Linear maps centred at an origin

anchor.snippet: Another important family of examples are the linear maps centred at an origin

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toAffineMap` reinterprets a linear map (fixing the origin) as an affine map.

anchors.snippet: Another important family of examples are the linear maps centred at an origin

annotations.label: Decomposition of affine map

anchor.snippet: any affine map may be written uniquely as a combination of a translation and a linear map centred at

annotations.note: `toConstProdLinearMap` is a linear equivalence `(V1 →ᵃ V2) ≃ V2 × (V1 →ₗ V2)`, giving the unique translation+linear decomposition (`AffineMap.decomp`).

annotations.label: Vector space as affine space

anchor.snippet: Every vector space V may be considered as an affine space over itself

annotations.note: `addGroupIsAddTorsor` makes any additive group (hence any vector space) a torsor over itself.

annotations.label: Origin choice induces isomorphism

anchor.snippet: the choice of any point a in A defines a unique affine isomorphism

annotations.note: `AffineEquiv.vaddConst b : V₁ ≃ᵃ[k] P₁` is the affine isomorphism `v ↦ v +ᵥ b` from choosing base point `b`.

annotations.label: Euclidean spaces are affine spaces

anchor.snippet: Euclidean spaces (including the one-dimensional line, two-dimensional plane

annotations.note: Euclidean geometry is developed over `[InnerProductSpace ℝ V] [NormedAddTorsor V P]`, i.e. affine spaces.

annotations.label: Euclidean space (modern)

anchor.snippet: a Euclidean space is defined to be an affine space, such that the associated vector space is a real inner product space

annotations.note: Mathlib's Euclidean setting is exactly an affine space `[NormedAddTorsor V P]` with `[InnerProductSpace ℝ V]`.

annotations.label: Euclidean space (synthetic, via equipollence)

anchor.snippet: In older definition of Euclidean spaces through synthetic geometry

annotations.note: The synthetic/equipollence-based definition of Euclidean space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine property

anchor.snippet: refers to a property that can be proved in affine spaces

annotations.note: There is no formal notion of an 'affine property' in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine property is transformation-invariant

anchor.snippet: an affine property is a property that is invariant under affine transformations

annotations.note: Invariance of affine properties under affine transformations is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Sum independent of origin

anchor.snippet: this sum is independent of the choice of the origin

annotations.note: `Finset.weightedVSub`/`affineCombination` are defined precisely so the result is independent of the base point when weights sum to 0 (resp. 1).

annotations.label: Barycenter / affine combination

anchor.snippet: is called the barycenter of the

annotations.note: `Finset.affineCombination` is the barycenter; `Finset.centroid` is the equal-weight case.

annotations.label: Number line

anchor.snippet: they are treating the number line as a one-dimensional affine space

annotations.note: ℝ is an affine space over itself via `addGroupIsAddTorsor`.

annotations.label: Time as affine space

anchor.snippet: Time can be modelled as a one-dimensional affine space

annotations.note: Modelling time as an affine space is a physical interpretation, not a formalized statement.

annotations.label: Space of energies

anchor.snippet: The space of energies is an affine space for

annotations.note: A physics example with no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Physical space

anchor.snippet: Physical space is often modelled as an affine space for

annotations.note: A physics modelling example, not a formalized statement.

annotations.label: Coset of a subspace

anchor.snippet: Any coset of a subspace V of a vector space is an affine space over that subspace

annotations.note: `mk' p direction` realizes the coset `p + V` as an affine subspace with direction `V`.

annotations.label: Line not through origin

anchor.snippet: a line in the plane that doesn't pass through the origin is an affine space that is not a vector space

annotations.note: Such a line is `affineSpan`/`line[k,p,q]`, an affine subspace, but the 'not a vector space' observation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Solution set of Tx = b

anchor.snippet: If T is a matrix and b lies in its column space

annotations.note: Solution sets are cosets and can be represented by `mk'`, but no lemma states that `{x | T x = b}` is an affine subspace.

annotations.label: Solutions of inhomogeneous ODE

anchor.snippet: The solutions of an inhomogeneous linear differential equation form an affine space

annotations.note: The affine structure on solutions of an inhomogeneous linear ODE is not formalized.

annotations.label: Solution set of linear map equation

anchor.snippet: if T : V → W is a linear map and y lies in its image

annotations.note: The preimage of a point under a linear map is a coset of the kernel (expressible via `mk'`), but not stated as a named affine-subspace result.

annotations.label: Complementary subspaces

anchor.snippet: The space of (linear) complementary subspaces of a vector subspace V in a vector space W is an affine space

annotations.note: The affine structure on the set of complements of a subspace is not formalized.

annotations.label: Space of connections

anchor.snippet: The space of connections (viewed from the vector bundle

annotations.note: Connections on vector bundles and their affine space structure are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine span

anchor.snippet: there is a smallest affine subspace that contains it, called the affine span of X

annotations.note: `affineSpan k s` is the smallest affine subspace containing `s` (it forms a `GaloisInsertion` with coercion).

annotations.label: Affine span as affine combinations

anchor.snippet: The affine span of X is the set of all (finite) affine combinations of points of X

annotations.note: `eq_affineCombination_of_mem_affineSpan` shows membership in the span is equivalent to being a finite affine combination.

annotations.label: Generating set

anchor.snippet: the affine span of X is generated by X and that X is a generating set of its affine span

annotations.note: Spanning is expressed by `affineSpan k s = ⊤`, but 'generating set' is not a named definition.

annotations.label: Affinely independent set

anchor.snippet: A set X of points of an affine space is said to be affinely independent

annotations.note: `AffineIndependent k p` states no nontrivial weighted subtraction (weights summing to 0) vanishes.

annotations.label: Affine basis

anchor.snippet: An affine basis or barycentric frame (see § Barycentric coordinates , below) of an affine space is a generating set that is also independent

annotations.note: `AffineBasis` is a family of affinely-independent points whose `affineSpan` is `⊤`.

annotations.label: Bases of finite-dimensional affine space

anchor.snippet: The bases of an affine space of finite dimension n are the independent subsets of n + 1 elements

annotations.note: This lemma shows `n+1` affinely-independent points in an `n`-dimensional space span the whole space.

annotations.label: Barycentric coordinates

anchor.snippet: are called the barycentric coordinates of x over the affine basis

annotations.note: `AffineBasis.coord i : P →ᵃ[k] k` is the i-th barycentric coordinate function.

annotations.label: Barycentric coordinate isomorphism

anchor.snippet: The barycentric coordinates define an affine isomorphism between the affine space A and the affine subspace

annotations.note: `coords` and `fintypeAffineCoords` are defined, but the affine equivalence between `P` and the coordinates summing to 1 is an explicit TODO in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Infinite-dimensional case

anchor.snippet: For affine spaces of infinite dimension, the same definition applies

annotations.note: `AffineBasis` is defined over an arbitrary index type with `finsuppAffineCoords`, but the infinite-dimensional coordinate isomorphism is not established.

annotations.label: Affine frame

anchor.snippet: An affine frame is a coordinate frame of an affine space

annotations.note: Affine frames (a base point plus a vector-space basis) are an explicit TODO in `AffineSpace.Defs` and not yet defined.

annotations.label: Affine coordinates

anchor.snippet: are called the affine coordinates of p over the affine frame

annotations.note: Barycentric coordinates (`AffineBasis.coord`) are formalized, but affine-frame coordinates (relative to a point and vector basis) are not.

annotations.label: Affine coordinate system

anchor.snippet: An affine coordinate system is a coordinate system on an affine space where each coordinate is an affine map to the number line

annotations.note: `AffineBasis.coord` gives coordinates as affine maps `P →ᵃ[k] k`, but only barycentric coordinate systems are formalized.

annotations.label: System of n coordinates

anchor.snippet: A system of n coordinates on n -dimensional space is defined by

annotations.note: `AffineBasis.coords : P →ᵃ[k] ι → k` gives a coordinate system, but the affine (non-barycentric) n-coordinate version is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lower bound on number of coordinates

anchor.snippet: An affine space cannot have a coordinate system with n less than its dimension

annotations.note: There is no formalized lower bound relating coordinate systems to affine dimension.

annotations.label: Cartesian coordinates

anchor.snippet: The most important case of affine coordinates in Euclidean spaces is the real-valued Cartesian coordinate system

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace`/orthonormal-basis coordinates exist, but a 'Cartesian coordinate system' is not defined as such.

annotations.label: Equivalence of barycentric and affine coordinates

anchor.section: Relationship between barycentric and affine coordinates
anchor.snippet: Barycentric coordinates and affine coordinates are strongly related, and may be considered as equivalent

annotations.note: The equivalence between barycentric and affine coordinates is not formalized (affine coordinates are absent).

annotations.label: Triangle as affine basis

anchor.snippet: The vertices of a non-flat triangle form an affine basis of the Euclidean plane

annotations.note: `Affine.Triangle = Affine.Simplex k P 2` is an affinely-independent family of three points, the affine basis of a plane.

annotations.label: Barycentric characterization of triangle

anchor.snippet: The vertices are the points of barycentric coordinates

annotations.note: `coord_apply_eq`/`coord_apply_ne` give that a basis point has barycentric coordinates `(0,…,1,…,0)`, of which the triangle vertices are an instance.

annotations.label: Change of barycentric basis

anchor.snippet: Barycentric coordinates are readily changed from one basis to another

annotations.note: `toMatrix_vecMul_coords` (and `toMatrix_inv_vecMul_coords`) give the change-of-basis formula for barycentric coordinates.

annotations.label: Change of affine basis

anchor.snippet: Affine coordinates are also readily changed from one basis to another

annotations.note: Change of basis is formalized for barycentric coordinates via `toMatrix`; the affine-coordinate change-of-frame formula is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Matrix representation of affine transformation

anchor.snippet: by a 4 by 4 matrix with a special

annotations.note: The augmented (homogeneous-coordinate) matrix representation of an affine transformation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Image of affine homomorphism

anchor.snippet: The image of f is the affine subspace

annotations.note: `AffineSubspace.map f` is the image affine subspace; the image of `f` is `(⊤).map f`.

annotations.label: Affine homomorphism has no kernel

anchor.snippet: an affine homomorphism does not have a kernel

annotations.note: This is a conceptual remark with no corresponding declaration.

annotations.label: Fiber

anchor.snippet: This affine subspace is called the fiber of x

annotations.note: A fiber is the `comap` of a singleton subspace; there is no dedicated `fiber` definition.

annotations.label: Projection parallel to a direction

anchor.snippet: the projection parallel to some direction onto an affine subspace

annotations.note: Only orthogonal projection onto an affine subspace is formalized; general projection parallel to an arbitrary direction is not.

annotations.label: Projection is affine homomorphism

anchor.snippet: This is an affine homomorphism whose associated linear map

annotations.note: `orthogonalProjection` is an affine map, but the general parallel-projection-is-affine statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Image and fibers of projection

anchor.snippet: The image of this projection is F

annotations.note: Image and fibers of a general parallel projection are not formalized.

annotations.label: Quotient space

anchor.snippet: The quotient E / D of E by D is the quotient of E by the equivalence relation

annotations.note: The quotient of an affine space by a direction submodule is not formalized (only linear `Submodule.Quotient` exists).

annotations.label: Quotient is an affine space

anchor.snippet: This quotient is an affine space

annotations.note: No affine-quotient construction or its affine-space structure is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem for affine spaces

anchor.snippet: This is the first isomorphism theorem for affine spaces

annotations.note: A first isomorphism theorem for affine spaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Coxeter axiomatization

anchor.snippet: axiomatizes the special case of affine geometry over the reals

annotations.note: Coxeter's synthetic axiomatization of real affine geometry is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine plane axioms

anchor.snippet: Affine planes satisfy the following axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib has projective-plane configurations but not the incidence axioms for affine planes.

annotations.label: Affine spaces inside projective spaces

anchor.snippet: Affine spaces are contained in projective spaces

annotations.note: `Projectivization` exists but the affine-chart embedding of an affine space into a projective space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine group as subgroup of projective group

anchor.snippet: any affine linear transformation extends uniquely to a projective linear transformation

annotations.note: The extension of affine transformations to projective ones is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine Möbius transformations

anchor.snippet: if and only if they fix the point at infinity

annotations.note: The characterization of affine maps as Möbius transformations fixing the point at infinity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine variety

anchor.snippet: an affine variety (or, more generally, an affine algebraic set ) is defined as the subset of an affine space

annotations.note: `MvPolynomial.zeroLocus I` is the common zero set in `σ → K` of an ideal of polynomials, i.e. an affine algebraic set.

annotations.label: Isomorphism with coordinate space

anchor.snippet: induces an affine isomorphism between

annotations.note: `𝔸(n; Spec R) ≅ Spec R[n]` is formalized scheme-theoretically, but the affine-geometry coordinate isomorphism `A ≅ kⁿ` is not packaged directly.

annotations.label: Affine spaces are affine varieties

anchor.snippet: affine spaces are affine algebraic varieties

annotations.note: The scheme `𝔸(n; S)` is defined and shown affine (`SpecIso`), but the precise 'affine space is an affine variety' identification is not isolated.

annotations.label: Polynomial functions form a k-algebra

anchor.snippet: is a k -algebra , denoted

annotations.note: `MvPolynomial σ k` carries `CommRing` and `Algebra k` instances, i.e. the polynomial k-algebra `k[X]`.

annotations.label: Total degree filtration

anchor.snippet: which is independent from the choice of coordinates

annotations.note: `totalDegree` is defined, but the induced filtration and its coordinate-independence are not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Zariski topology

anchor.snippet: Zariski topology is the unique topology on an affine space whose closed sets are affine algebraic sets

annotations.note: The Zariski topology is formalized on the prime spectrum (closed sets are `zeroLocus`); the version directly on affine space `kⁿ` is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Zariski coarser than natural topology

anchor.snippet: Zariski topology is coarser than the natural topology

annotations.note: The comparison of the Zariski topology with the metric/natural topology on affine space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Injection into spectrum

anchor.snippet: There is a natural injective function from an affine space into the set of prime ideals

annotations.note: Points correspond to maximal ideals via `vanishingIdeal` of singletons, capturing the injection's content, but it is not packaged as a `PrimeSpectrum`-valued map.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism onto image

anchor.snippet: This function is a homeomorphism

annotations.note: The homeomorphism of affine space onto its image in the prime spectrum is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hilbert's Nullstellensatz

anchor.snippet: this is Hilbert's Nullstellensatz

annotations.note: `vanishingIdeal_zeroLocus_eq_radical` is the classical Nullstellensatz: `I(V(I)) = radical I`.

annotations.label: Cohomology of affine space is trivial

anchor.snippet: the cohomology of affine space is trivial

annotations.note: Triviality of (quasi-coherent) cohomology of affine space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Trivial étale cohomology

anchor.snippet: all of the étale cohomology groups on affine space are trivial

annotations.note: Étale cohomology of affine space is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quillen–Suslin theorem

anchor.snippet: the Quillen–Suslin theorem implies that every algebraic vector bundle over an affine space is trivial

annotations.note: The Quillen–Suslin theorem is not in Mathlib (matches for 'Quillen'/'Suslin' refer to unrelated model-category and descriptive-set-theory uses).
